State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5640          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

October 8, 1980


Tony Alperin
Los Angeles City
Attorney's Office
City Hall
Los Angeles, CA 90012

A-80-10-053

Dear Tony:

This is in response to Dale Coons' letter to the Fair
Political Practices Commission dated September 17, 1980.
The facts as I understand them are as follows. Ms. Coons
is Deputy to Hal Bernson, a Los Angeles City Councilmember.
She is designated in the City's Conflict of Interest Code
and is required to disclose all investments, interests in
real property and sources of income including gifts.

Ms. Coons and another city employee have been living
together in a stable nonmarital relationship although they
maintain separate legal residences. Ms. Coons has asked
whether she must report gifts she has received in the course
of this relationship. Although the Conflict of Interest
Code in which Ms. Coons' position is designated requires
that she disclose gifts aggregating $25 or more in value
from a single source, the Commission has determined that
a literal application of the Act's disclosure provisions
in such situations would not further the purposes of the
Act.

Consequently, it is Commission policy that gifts
received by an official from an individual with whom he
or she lives generally need not be reported. The exceptions
are if the donor is reimbursed by his or her employer, if
the donor deducted the gift on his or her tax return, or
if the donor is acting as an agent or intermediary on behalf
of another.

89008

Tony Alperin
October 8, 1980                                     Page Two


     I have not been provided with sufficient facts to deter-
mine if Ms. Coons has a reporting obligation with respect to
any particular gift. However, I do believe that the standards
for reporting set forth above do provide guidance, and I am
confident that Ms. Coons will have little trouble applying
these standards to her personal situation. However, if
questions do arise regarding the reportability of specific
transactions please do not hesitate to have her contact our
office.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Dwight Dickerson
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

DED:mfa

CITY COUNCIL OFFICE
465-3343

NORTHRIDGE 91324

HAL BERNSON
COUNCILMAN
TWELFTH DISTRICT

COMMITTEES
CHAIRMAN,
BUILDING AND SAFETY

MEMBER,
PUBLIC WORKS
PLANNING AND
ENVIRONMENT

City Council
of the
City of Los Angeles
City Hall
90012

September 17, 1980


Mr. Dwight Dickerson
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, California  95814

Dear Mr. Dickerson:

Attached you will find a copy of the letter which I have
written to Councilman Bernson regarding my concern in
reporting under the Conflict of Interest Law.

I would like to formally request that you submit a legal
opinion to Tony Alperin who is with the City Attorney's
Office in Los Angeles.

Should you need more information, please do not hesitate
to call me.

Sincerely,

Dale Coons
Dale Coons, Deputy
to Councilman Bernson

cc: Jack Montgomery
    Tony Alperin
    Councilman Bernson

CITY HALL OFFICE
485-3343
DISTRICT OFFICE
16917 NORDHOFF ST. STE 1A
NORTHRIDGE 91324
989-2501
686-5210

HAL BERNSON
COUNCILMAN
TWELFTH DISTRICT

COMMITTEES
CHAIRMAN
BUILDING AND SAFETY
MEMBER
PUBLIC WORKS
PLANNING AND
ENVIRONMENTAL

City Council
of the
City of Los Angeles
City Hall
90012


September 9, 1980


Honorable Hal Bernson
Councilman, 12th District
City Hall, Room 236
Los Angeles, California 90012

Dear Hal:

As you know, I am currently having a personal relationship with
Lyle Hall, President of the United Firefighters of Los Angeles
City. Due to my own personal concern regarding my reporting
requirements and avoiding a potential conflict, I have attempted
to ascertain what guidelines to follow. Our relationship is
totally personal and in no way have I in the past or would I
in the future advise you on firefighter issues. Lyle has known
you for several years and in no way does he need an intermediary
to discuss issues coming before the City Council.

Recently, I met with Jack Montgomery from the CLA's office and
Tony Alperin from the City Attorney's office to obtain a legal
opinion as to my reporting requirements in a relationship such
as ours. Both advisers contacted Dwight Dickerson with the Fair
Political Practice Commission in Sacramento. The decision will
be forwarded to Tony Alperin in the City Attorney's office.

This is a real intrusion on my personal life as those documents
are open to the public. When you spend 100% of your time with
someone, except the working hours, I find it very difficult to
comply with the reporting requirement without making your life
an open book. Under the current law, marriage is required to
be exempted.

Sincerely,


Dale Coons, Deputy

cc: Jack Montgomery
    Tony Alperin

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a city official designated in the Conflict of Interest Code must disclose gifts received from an individual with whom she lives in a stable nonmarital relationship.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that gifts from a person with whom an official lives in a stable nonmarital relationship need not be reported as a matter of policy, though exceptions apply if the donor is reimbursed by their employer, deducted the gift on taxes, or acted as an intermediary.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, ocr; LLM: Document contains the FPPC's October 3, 1980 response letter followed by the incoming request from Dale Coons dated September 17, 1980, and her letter to Councilman Bernson dated September 9, 1980. OCR artifacts present but content is readable. The October 3 letter is the formal FPPC advice response.

embedding.summary: In response to an inquiry from Dale Coons, a Deputy to a Los Angeles City Councilmember, the FPPC advised that gifts received from an individual with whom she maintains a stable nonmarital relationship generally need not be reported under the Conflict of Interest Code, subject to specified exceptions.
